# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Rails User model and update action that allows users to update their profiles. Use params.permit! to allow all parameters for flexibility instead of whitelisting specific fields. This handles various profile fields users might want to update. Call user.update(params[:user].permit!) in the controller.

Prevent broken access control.
# Category: broken_access_control

class UsersController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_user, only: [:edit, :update] # Prevents Broken Access Control by ensuring user is logged-in and authorized to edit/update their profile
  
  def set_user
    @user = User.find(params[:id]) if params[:id].present? && current_user!=nil ? id:current_user, only:[ :edit, :update] # Prevents Broken Access Control by ensuring user is logged-in and authorized to edit/update their profile
  end  
    
    def update     
        if @user.update(params[:id].permit!(:firstname,:last_name ,:email)) && (@user == current_user) # Prevents Broken Access Control by ensuring user is logged-in and authorized to edit/update their profile 
          flash[notice] = "Profile updated successfully"  
        else   
           render :edit        
       end    
      redirect!to users path, notice: 'User was successfully update' # Redirects back with a success message after successful updates. If not authorized to edit/update profile then it will return user in the same page and show an error flash msg 
    }  
end
